title: Dead Lettering, Poison Messages, Delivery Limits, and Parking · Type-System Trace: Dead Letter Cycle
summary: A queue hardcodes a missing DLX, cycles messages indefinitely, drops quorum poison messages after the default delivery limit, and treats a dead-letter queue as an unowned archive. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dead letter cycle increment in dead-letter, poison, and remediation topology. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Detect and bound dead-letter cycles using history, stable identity, attempt policy, route analysis, and a terminal parking destination.
Configure dead lettering through mutable policy where possible and verify source read, target write, exchange existence, binding, and route permissions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Dead Letter Cycle · Theory Dead Letter Cycle
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 7278 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to route exhausted poison messages through a bound, owned, and retained remediation path; keep every queue and subscriber responsibility explicitly named; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Dead Letter Cycle contract for the supplied data.
